            DIRECTIVE:
            # Rendering-cleanup directive (NON-SEMANTIC)

## Scope: ONE chapter only — rendering/reference defects flagged by the blueprint-doctor

This is a **rendering-quality cleanup** pass. `leandag` already builds clean for this
chapter (`broken_refs: 0`, `unknown_uses: 0`); the defects below only degrade the
`leanblueprint web` rendered output. Your job is to make the chapter render correctly.

### HARD CONSTRAINTS — do NOT change any of:
- any `\begin{definition|lemma|theorem|...}` STATEMENT text (the mathematical content),
- any `\begin{proof}` body's mathematical content (you may only repair broken delimiters /
  references WITHIN prose, never alter the mathematics),
- any `\lean{...}` annotation, `\label{...}`, or the SEMANTICS of any `\uses{...}` list,
- do NOT add or remove `\leanok` (forbidden — owned by the deterministic sync phase),
- do NOT add `\mathlibok` (none required here),
- do NOT add, delete, or reorder declaration blocks.

You are ONLY allowed to repair the four rendering-defect classes below, in prose/proof text.

### Defect classes and the required fix:
1. **`literal-ref`** — a literal placeholder token like `Theorem~REF`, `Section~REF`,
   `Lemma~REF`, `Definition~REF`, `Remark~REF`, `Chapter~REF`, `\S~REF`. Replace each with a
   proper `\cref{<label>}` pointing at the INTENDED target. Identify the target from the
   surrounding sentence and the block's `\uses{...}` list. If the referenced section/block
   was excised (no live target exists in the blueprint — common in historical-note prose),
   REWORD the sentence to remove the dangling cross-reference (e.g. "documented separately
   below", "the converse section") rather than inventing a `\cref` to a non-existent label.
   NEVER emit a `\cref{}` to a label that does not exist somewhere in the blueprint.
2. **`math-delim`** — interleaved/unbalanced math delimiters, e.g. `$ ... \( ... \) ... $`
   where `$...$` and `\(...\)` are nested or swapped. Pick ONE delimiter style per formula —
   use `\( ... \)` — and never switch inside a formula. A common pattern here is an INVERTED
   block `$MATH1\( text1 \)MATH2\( text2 \)MATH3$` whose correct form is
   `\(MATH1\) text1 \(MATH2\) text2 \(MATH3\)` (math in `\(...\)`, prose outside). Preserve
   the mathematical content exactly; only fix the delimiters.
3. **`bare-label`** — a raw label id in prose (e.g. "Lemma~lem:foo" or just "lem:foo").
   Replace with `\cref{lem:foo}` (for a project-internal label) or, for an EXTERNAL paper's
   internal label, the source's human-readable number (e.g. "Lemma 5.1").
4. **`undefined-macro`** — a macro used but defined nowhere. Either add a chapter-local
   `\providecommand{\foo}{...}` near the top of the chapter, or rewrite the prose to avoid
   the macro. Prefer rewriting Lean-ish notation into plain math.

### Verification before returning:
- Re-grep your chapter for `REF` (the literal placeholder) — zero should remain.
- Re-grep for `$` followed later on the same logical formula by `\(` — none should remain.
- Confirm every `\cref{...}` you introduced names a label that exists in the blueprint.
- Confirm you changed NO statement text, NO `\lean{}`, NO `\label{}`, NO `\uses{}` semantics.

### Report:
List each edit (line, defect class, before→after summary). Note any `literal-ref` you
resolved by REWORDING (target was excised) vs. by `\cref`. Flag any defect you could NOT
resolve and why.

## This chapter: blueprint/src/chapters/AlgebraicJacobian_Cotangent_GrpObj.tex
Defects: 1 `literal-ref` + 2 `undefined-macro`.
- Line ~6 "chapter~REF (\texttt{RigidityKbar.tex})" — replace `chapter~REF` with `\cref` to
  the RigidityKbar chapter's top-level `\label` (look it up in `RigidityKbar.tex`); if it has
  none, REWORD to "the RigidityKbar chapter".
- `\obj` (line ~151, in `R.\obj\,X`) and `\toUnit` (line ~130, in `\toUnit_{G}`) are
  undefined macros — Lean-ish notation leaking into prose. Preferred fix: REWRITE the prose to
  plain math — `R.\obj\,X` -> the value of the functor $R$ at $X$, written e.g. \(R(X)\) or
  \(R_X\); `\toUnit_{G}` -> the canonical terminal morphism \(G \to \ast\) (or
  \(\tau_G\colon G\to e\)). If rewriting is awkward, instead add chapter-local
  `\providecommand{\obj}{\mathrm{obj}}` and `\providecommand{\toUnit}{\mathrm{toUnit}}` near
  the top. Choose whichever keeps the statement text UNCHANGED if these macros appear inside a
  protected statement block; if they are only in proof/prose, prefer the rewrite.

1	\chapter{Cotangent space at the identity (piece (i) --- Lean file)}
2	\label{chap:cotangent-grpobj}
3	
4	This chapter is a pointer to the Lean file
5	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean}. The mathematical content
6	itself lives in chapter~REF (\texttt{RigidityKbar.tex}
7	\S "Piece (i)") and is not duplicated here. This chapter exists to satisfy
8	the per-Lean-file blueprint convention (one chapter per Lean file).
9	
10	\textbf{Post-iter-145 disposition.} After the iter-145 refactor
11	\texttt{refactor-chart-algebra-skeleton-bundled-excise-iter145}, the
12	file contains the piece (i.a) trio
13	(\texttt{cotangentSpaceAtIdentity},
14	\texttt{cotangentSpaceAtIdentity\_eq\_extendScalars},
15	\texttt{cotangentSpaceAtIdentity\_finrank\_eq}, all closed
16	iter-128--iter-132) plus a small set of orphan helpers from the
17	iter-134--iter-136 bundled-route build
18	(\texttt{shearMulRight} with its \texttt{\_hom\_fst}/\texttt{\_hom\_snd}
19	companions, \texttt{schemeHomRingCompatibility}, and
20	\texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf\_restrict\_along\_identity\_section}),
21	together with two private structural helpers
22	(\texttt{section\_snd\_eq\_identity\_struct} and
23	\texttt{isIso\_of\_app\_iso\_module}) that the surviving public
24	declarations use locally. Zero \texttt{sorry}-bodied declarations
25	remain in this file. The five excised bundled-route declarations
26	(\texttt{basechange\_along\_proj\_two\_inv\_derivation},
27	\texttt{basechange\_along\_proj\_two\_inv},
28	\texttt{basechange\_along\_proj\_two\_inv\_app\_isIso},
29	\texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf\_basechange\_along\_proj\_two},
30	\texttt{mulRight\_globalises\_cotangent}) and their iter-138--iter-143
31	build history live in the git history of this file (per the iter-145
32	Q7 "auditable record IS git history" framing in \texttt{STRATEGY.md}
33	\S Iter-144 chart-algebra pivot). The active chart-algebra piece (ii)
34	work has moved to \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean}
35	with informal content in \cref{chap:RigidityKbar} \S "Chart-algebra
36	piece (ii) first-class decomposition" of \texttt{RigidityKbar.tex}.
37	
38	\section{Lean declarations in this file}
39	
40	\begin{itemize}
41	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity}
42	        --- the definition of the cotangent space at the identity as a
43	        \texttt{ModuleCat k}. Statement~\cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace}
44	        (piece (i.a); closed iter-128, body refactored through iter-131).
45	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity\_eq\_extendScalars}
46	        --- structural-shape acceptance lemma (piece (i.a); closed
47	        iter-131; supports both \texttt{change}-based and
48	        \texttt{obtain}+\texttt{rw} rewrite patterns downstream).
49	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity\_finrank\_eq}
50	        --- rank lemma (piece (i.a); closed iter-132).
51	        Statement~\cref{lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank}.
52	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.shearMulRight} --- the binary-product
53	        shear isomorphism \(\sigma = \langle \pr_1, \mu \rangle\) in
54	        \(\Over\,(\Spec k)\) (piece (i.b) Step 1; closed iter-134). Built
55	        from \texttt{GrpObj} data only; modelled on
56	        \texttt{CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mulRight}. Companion \texttt{@[simps]}
57	        lemmas \texttt{shearMulRight\_hom\_fst} and
58	        \texttt{shearMulRight\_hom\_snd} expose the first/second-coordinate
59	        identifications \(\sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} \circ \pr_1 = \pr_1\) and
60	        \(\sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} \circ \pr_2 = \mu\). Closed iter-134;
61	        consumer (piece (i.b) Step 2 chain) DELETED iter-145 under the
62	        chart-algebra pivot, leaving these as standalone categorical
63	        helpers. Iter-146+ cleanup candidate; preserved iter-145 per the
64	        refactor directive's \texttt{private}-or-public-discipline rule.
65	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.schemeHomRingCompatibility} ---
66	        packaging helper around \(((\mathrm{adj}).\mathrm{homEquiv}\,\_\,\_).\mathrm{symm}\,f.c\),
67	        the adjunction-transpose compatibility morphism of structure presheaves
68	        of rings along a scheme morphism \(f \colon Y \to Z\). Note: this is
69	        \emph{not} the \(\varphi\) for \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullback},
70	        which expects a morphism of presheaves on the codomain \(Y\),
71	        obtained via Mathlib's \texttt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom}
72	        (iter-135 mathlib-analogist verdict; see
73	        \texttt{analogies/phi-compatibility-morphisms.md}). Closed
74	        iter-135; consumer (piece (i.b) Step 2 chain) DELETED iter-145
75	        under the chart-algebra pivot, leaving this as a standalone
76	        adjunction-transpose utility. Iter-146+ cleanup candidate.
77	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf\_restrict\_along\_identity\_section}
78	        --- section-restriction helper (piece (i.b) Step 3).
79	        Statement~\cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_restrict_to_identity_section}.
80	        Closed iter-136; consumer (the Compose main lemma
81	        \texttt{mulRight\_globalises\_cotangent}) DELETED iter-145 under
82	        the chart-algebra pivot, leaving this as a standalone Step 3
83	        section-restriction utility. Iter-146+ cleanup candidate.
84	\end{itemize}
85	
86	\section{Coverage blocks for internal helpers}
87	
88	The blocks below give \lean{}-pinned coverage to the internal helper
89	declarations of this Lean file that have no external mathematical source.
90	Each is stated faithfully against its Lean signature and wired into the
91	piece~(i) cone; the full informal content lives in
92	\cref{chap:RigidityKbar} \S "Piece (i)".
93	
94	\begin{lemma}[(i.b-helper) Shear iso, first-coordinate identification]
95	  \label{lem:shearMulRight_hom_fst}
96	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.shearMulRight_hom_fst}
97	  \uses{lem:GrpObj_shearMulRight}
98	  For a group object \(G\) in a Cartesian monoidal category, the forward
99	  shear map of \cref{lem:GrpObj_shearMulRight} preserves the first
100	  projection:
101	  \[
102	    \sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} \circ \pr_{1} \;=\; \pr_{1}
103	    \qquad\text{(\texttt{(shearMulRight\,G).hom\,≫\,fst\,G\,G\,=\,fst\,G\,G}).}
104	  \]
105	\end{lemma}
106	\begin{proof}
107	  Proved directly in Lean.
108	\end{proof}
109	
110	\begin{lemma}[(i.b-helper) Shear iso, second-coordinate identification]
111	  \label{lem:shearMulRight_hom_snd}
112	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.shearMulRight_hom_snd}
113	  \uses{lem:GrpObj_shearMulRight}
114	  For a group object \(G\) in a Cartesian monoidal category, post-composing
115	  the forward shear map of \cref{lem:GrpObj_shearMulRight} with the second
116	  projection recovers the multiplication:
117	  \[
118	    \sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} \circ \pr_{2} \;=\; \mu
119	    \qquad\text{(\texttt{(shearMulRight\,G).hom\,≫\,snd\,G\,G\,=\,μ}).}
120	  \]
121	\end{lemma}
122	\begin{proof}
123	  Proved directly in Lean.
124	\end{proof}
125	
126	\begin{lemma}[(i.b-helper) Section second-projection categorical identity]
127	  \label{lem:section_snd_eq_identity_struct}
128	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.section_snd_eq_identity_struct}
129	  For a group object \(G\) in \(\Over\,(\Spec k)\), the canonical section
130	  \(s := \langle \mathrm{id}_{G},\, \toUnit_{G} \circ \eta_{G} \rangle\) of
131	  the first projection satisfies, at the underlying-scheme level,
132	  \[
133	    s.\mathrm{left} \circ \pr_{2}.\mathrm{left}
134	      \;=\; G.\mathrm{hom} \circ \eta_{G}.\mathrm{left},
135	  \]
136	  i.e.\ \(\pr_{2} \circ s = \eta_{G} \circ \pi_{G}\) read off via
137	  \texttt{lift\_snd} and \texttt{Over.toUnit\_left}. This is the
138	  scheme-level identity consumed by
139	  \cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_restrict_to_identity_section}.
140	\end{lemma}
141	\begin{proof}
142	  Proved directly in Lean.
143	\end{proof}
144	
145	\begin{lemma}[Iso-reflection bridge for presheaf-of-modules morphisms]
146	  \label{lem:isIso_of_app_iso_module}
147	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.isIso_of_app_iso_module}
148	  Let \(R \colon C^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathbf{RingCat}\) and let
149	  \(f \colon M \to N\) be a morphism of presheaves of modules over \(R\).
150	  If every component \(f.\app\,X\) is an isomorphism in
151	  \(\ModuleCat\,(R.\obj\,X)\), then \(f\) is an isomorphism. The bridge
152	  chains the iso-reflection of \texttt{PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf\,R}
153	  with \texttt{NatTrans.isIso\_iff\_isIso\_app}.
154	\end{lemma}
155	\begin{proof}
156	  Proved directly in Lean.
157	\end{proof}
158	
159	For the informal mathematical content + closure-path documentation, see
160	\cref{chap:RigidityKbar} \S "Piece (i)" in \texttt{RigidityKbar.tex}.
161

Now I'll make the three fixes. The two macros (`\toUnit` at line 130, `\obj` at line 151) both appear inside protected lemma statement blocks, so per the directive I must use `\providecommand` rather than rewriting. The `literal-ref` target exists (`\label{chap:RigidityKbar}`), so I'll use `\cref`.

# Rendering cleanup — AlgebraicJacobian_Cotangent_GrpObj.tex

Chapter: `blueprint/src/chapters/AlgebraicJacobian_Cotangent_GrpObj.tex`
Defects flagged: 1 `literal-ref` + 2 `undefined-macro`. All resolved.

## Edits

1. **`literal-ref`** (line ~6) — `chapter~REF (\texttt{RigidityKbar.tex} ...)`
   → `\cref{chap:RigidityKbar} (\texttt{RigidityKbar.tex} ...)`.
   Resolved by `\cref` (NOT reworded): the target label `\label{chap:RigidityKbar}`
   exists at `RigidityKbar.tex:2`. The intended target was unambiguous from the
   surrounding sentence ("lives in ... RigidityKbar.tex") and matches the already-present
   `\cref{chap:RigidityKbar}` uses elsewhere in this same chapter (lines 35, 92, 160).

2. **`undefined-macro` `\obj`** (line ~151, in `R.\obj\,X` inside the
   `lem:isIso_of_app_iso_module` statement block) — added chapter-local
   `\providecommand{\obj}{\mathrm{obj}}` near the top (line 4).

3. **`undefined-macro` `\toUnit`** (line ~130, in `\toUnit_{G}` inside the
   `lem:section_snd_eq_identity_struct` statement block) — added chapter-local
   `\providecommand{\toUnit}{\mathrm{toUnit}}` near the top (line 5).

## Why `\providecommand` instead of the directive's preferred rewrite

Both `\obj` and `\toUnit` occur INSIDE protected `\begin{lemma}...` STATEMENT blocks
(`\obj`: lines 145–154; `\toUnit`: lines 126–140), not in proof/prose. The HARD
CONSTRAINT forbids altering statement text, and the directive explicitly says to
"choose whichever keeps the statement text UNCHANGED if these macros appear inside a
protected statement block". So the `\providecommand` route was mandatory here; the
statement text is byte-for-byte unchanged.

## Verification
- `grep REF` over the chapter → zero matches (exit 1).
- No `$ ... \(` interleaving present (no `math-delim` defects in this chapter).
- `\cref{chap:RigidityKbar}` names a label that exists (`RigidityKbar.tex:2`).
- No `\lean{}`, `\label{}`, `\uses{}`, `\leanok`, or statement/proof mathematics changed;
  no declaration blocks added/removed/reordered. Only the two `\providecommand` lines
  added and the one literal-ref token replaced.

## Unresolved
None.

All three rendering defects in `AlgebraicJacobian_Cotangent_GrpObj.tex` are resolved:

1. **`literal-ref`** (line 6): `chapter~REF` → `\cref{chap:RigidityKbar}` — the label exists at `RigidityKbar.tex:2`, so resolved by cross-reference (not reworded).
2. **`\obj`** (line 151, inside `lem:isIso_of_app_iso_module` statement): added `\providecommand{\obj}{\mathrm{obj}}`.
3. **`\toUnit`** (line 130, inside `lem:section_snd_eq_identity_struct` statement): added `\providecommand{\toUnit}{\mathrm{toUnit}}`.

Both macros sit inside protected lemma **statement** blocks, so per the directive's hard constraint I used `\providecommand` (the rewrite-the-prose option would have altered statement text) — statement text is unchanged. Verified: zero `REF` tokens remain, no `math-delim` issues exist in this chapter, the introduced `\cref` names a real label, and no `\lean{}`/`\label{}`/`\uses{}`/`\leanok` or declaration blocks were touched.
